methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Cascade Space is a seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Jacob Portukalian and Arlen Abraham that has raised $5.9M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparables universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin, though meaningful peer multiples are constrained by limited same-stage data density within the narrow space subsector.

Stage-progression analysis is the sole applicable method. Using total capital raised ($6M proxy for current implied value) and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement, the method implies a valuation of $15M. Public-comp anchoring—where Space & Aerospace trades at a median 53.2x P/S across the five public names—cannot be applied without the target's revenue, which is not available. Peer-median triangulation also does not meet the minimum threshold of three comparable datapoints at the same stage or adjacent valuation tier within the sector.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $13M to $17M, with a median of $15M. This range reflects mechanical application of a single method and carries material uncertainty. Key gaps include the lack of revenue disclosure (blocking public-comp validation) and sparse seed-stage private comparables in space, limiting confidence in the step-up multiplier itself. Additional disclosure of runway, burn rate, or technology stage would strengthen conviction.
